title: Blephariglottis lacera
intro: Blephariglottis lacera, also known as the 'cut-leaved Indian gentian', is a plant species native to the Himalayan region and parts of Asia, belonging to the Gentianaceae family. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and act as a natural remedy for various gastrointestinal problems. Bioactive constituents of Blephariglottis lacera include alkaloids, glycosides, and flavonoids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as decoctions, infusions, and powders, often used to treat coughs, colds, and digestive issues.
taxonomy: Blephariglottis lacera is classified within the family Orchidaceae, which is the largest family of flowering plants. It is a genus of orchids native to the Himalayas. The specific classification of Blephariglottis lacera includes the kingdom Plantae, and further categorization into the division Magnoliophyta, class Liliopsida, and order Asparagales. Within the family Orchidaceae, Blephariglottis lacera is a member of the subfamily Orchidoideae. Its classification is also known as Blephariglottis lacera, being a part of the subtribe Coelogyniinae.
